vulnerability.reference: Model weights retain speaker-specific acoustic patterns after unlearning, enabling re-identification from outputs.
vulnerability.generated: Data subjects lack independent oversight to verify complete voice identity removal, relying solely on the provider's unverified unlearning claim.

impact.reference: Re-identification of data subjects by querying model with various prompts.
impact.generated: Re-identification and voice cloning of deletion-requesting individuals, enabling impersonation and privacy violations despite legal deletion rights.

dose_response.reference: Incomplete unlearning leaves residual voiceprint patterns in model weights; when providers apply unlearning for deletion requests, data subjects can be re-identified via model queries, leading to regulatory fines and reputational damage.
dose_response.generated: Incomplete speaker identity unlearning in zero-shot TTS, through data subjects exercising their right to be forgotten, triggers lack of independent oversight to verify identity removal, leading to re-identification and voice cloning enabling impersonation and privacy violations despite legal deletion rights.
